summary.intent: Run local validation (`./scripts/local-validate.sh`) on the moltis project and fix all failures to get a clean CI-equivalent check locally.
summary.outcome: All identified failures were fixed across multiple iterations: rustfmt formatting was applied, a bash 3.2 compatibility issue in `check-file-size.sh` was resolved, and three Rust compile errors (missing struct field, missing method parameters, wrong trait signature) were corrected. The fmt check continued to fail after each code fix due to formatter drift, requiring repeated rustfmt runs.

repo: local-validate.sh runs checks via `bash -lc` which resolves to /bin/bash 3.2 on macOS, so scripts invoked by it must be bash 3.2 compatible (no `declare -A` associative arrays).
`ChannelMessageMeta` struct has an `agent_id` field that must be explicitly set (e.g., `None`) in all initializers.
`ApprovalBroadcaster::broadcast_request` and `ApprovalManager::create_request` both require a `session_key: Option<&str>` parameter that was recently added to the trait/method signatures.
The project uses nightly rustfmt (`cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt`) and any new code added without running it will fail the fmt check.
The file-size check allowlists files up to 1500 lines (42 files allowlisted as of this session).

code.finding: ChannelMessageMeta initializer was missing the `agent_id` field; added `agent_id: None`.
Calls to `create_request` and `broadcast_request` were missing the new `session_key: Option<&str>` argument; passed `None` for both (filesystem approvals are not session-scoped).
TestBroadcaster's `broadcast_request` impl was missing the `_session_key: Option<&str>` parameter required by the updated trait definition.
`declare -A` (associative array) is bash 4+ only; fails under macOS system bash 3.2 when invoked via `bash -lc` from local-validate.sh. Replaced with a loop-based approach.

workflow: After fixing any Rust code, always re-run `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all` before committing, as the nightly formatter will reformat newly added code and cause a fmt check failure.
The local-validate.sh script runs checks in parallel and reports per-check timing; failures in one check do not stop other checks from completing.
Compile errors cascade: fixing one crate's error may reveal new errors in dependent crates on the next run.

friction: The fmt check failed on every iteration after a code fix because nightly rustfmt reformats newly written code, requiring a separate rustfmt pass after each fix — no single-pass resolution.
local-validate.sh uses `bash -lc` which silently switches to /bin/bash 3.2 on macOS, causing cryptic `declare: -A: invalid option` errors that don't clearly identify the root cause.

Compile errors were revealed incrementally across multiple `./scripts/local-validate.sh` runs (nostr crate error, then tools crate errors), requiring multiple fix-and-rerun cycles rather than a single batch fix.
open_items: Confirm the full `./scripts/local-validate.sh` suite passes after the final rustfmt run — the session ends before a clean full run is confirmed.
Consider adding a shebang or explicit bash version guard to scripts invoked by local-validate.sh to prevent future bash 3.2 vs 4+ compatibility surprises.
The nostr crate's `agent_id: None` default may need revisiting if agent context becomes available in that code path.
